posts.title: Records a group can keep — without any one keyholder becoming a target 🔐
posts.ogTitle: Cryptographic RBAC over a CRDT — records a group can keep without any one keyholder becoming a target
posts.excerpt: Cryptographic role-based access control over a local-first CRDT database — records a group can keep together, merged for local and group consensus, with Shamir threshold custody so no single keyholder is ever worth coercing. A tour of the datalayer, plus a live three-user demo.

posts.title: One HTML file vs. a web application: sample intake without the IT-approval gauntlet 📄
posts.ogTitle: One HTML file vs. a web application: sample intake without the IT-approval gauntlet
posts.excerpt: Web apps are easy to build; getting IT to approve them is not. A single HTML file is the free intermediate step — offline, no server, but with SQLite, Web Crypto, and a scannable QR that composes your form data into a label so transcription never has to happen.

posts.title: RheoServ Waumpaum — a credit-union core in one HTML file 🏦
posts.ogTitle: RheoServ Waumpaum — a credit-union core in one HTML file
posts.excerpt: A sovereign, offline-first counterpart to enterprise core banking: a person-centered credit-union core in one encrypted HTML file, with CRDT multi-operator merge and waumpaum settlement between institutions. Teller, merge, and payment-provider walkthroughs with videos.

posts.title: Hiding plastron links in pictures with ChaosEdgeSteg 🐢
posts.ogTitle: Hiding plastron links in pictures with ChaosEdgeSteg
posts.excerpt: Chaos-based edge steganography from crypt0lith, running verbatim in Pyodide cells. Hide a plastron.ca #f= link in a Twitter-safe PNG; recover it with cover + password.

posts.title: Offline HRIS for a community police office 👮
posts.ogTitle: Offline HRIS for a community police office
posts.excerpt: A plastron detour — CRDT SQLite in one HTML file for offline HR merge. Juárez-skinned demo; patterns intended for plastron's own offline-capable future.

posts.title: 曳尾塗中 — drag your tail in the mud 🐢
posts.ogTitle: 曳尾塗中 — drag your tail in the mud
posts.excerpt: Zhuangzi's tortoise story in traditional Chinese. Click any character to break it down through a decomposition tree with English glosses.
